---
title: "Installation"
---

## Prerequisites

### Python

Python **≥ 3.11** is required.

### Quarto

Install [Quarto](https://quarto.org/docs/get-started/) for your platform.
`quarto-coursegen generate` only writes plain `.qmd` files and does not invoke
Quarto, but you will need it for the rendering stage.

### Quarto extensions

The built-in `.qmd.j2` templates reference the
[fontawesome](https://github.com/quarto-ext/fontawesome) extension.
Install it once per course project after running `quarto-coursegen init`:

```bash
quarto add quarto-ext/fontawesome
```

## Installing quarto-coursegen

### Via pip

```bash
pip install quarto-coursegen
```

### Via uv (recommended)

[uv](https://docs.astral.sh/uv/) installs `quarto-coursegen` as an isolated
CLI tool, available globally without activating a virtual environment:

```bash
uv tool install quarto-coursegen
```

To update later:

```bash
uv tool upgrade quarto-coursegen
```

### Verify the installation

```bash
quarto-coursegen --version
```

## Python virtual environments

If you prefer a project-local install, create a virtual environment first:

```bash
python -m venv .venv
source .venv/bin/activate   # Windows: .venv\Scripts\activate
pip install quarto-coursegen
```

## Upgrading

```bash
pip install --upgrade quarto-coursegen
# or
uv tool upgrade quarto-coursegen
```
